reasoning: Polymarket acquired a CFTC-regulated DCM (QCX) for $112M and received the 'green light' to launch US operations, intending to begin listing contracts as early as October 2, 2025. However, the launch has been delayed primarily by a US government shutdown that began on October 1, 2025, halting most CFTC operations (including self-certifications). While this shutdown is ongoing as of late October 2025 (knowledge cutoff), it is highly unlikely to last until December 31, 2025 (which would make it a 92-day shutdown, significantly longer than the historical record of 35 days). Once the government reopens, CFTC functions will resume, and Polymarket is poised to immediately list its pre-filed athletic and election contracts. Given the monumental financial backing (e.g., a $2B investment from the ICE/NYSE owner) and explicit plans to tie a token airdrop to the US launch, Polymarket has overwhelmingly strong incentives to ensure a real-money trade is executed before year-end. A residual risk remains that an unprecedentedly long shutdown or unexpected last-minute regulatory blockers from the CFTC could delay the launch until 2026, keeping the probability slightly below certainty.
